factors.summary: The company is focused on increasing market share through strategic initiatives and acquisitions.

evidence.text: We believe we are well-positioned to take advantage of the construction activity in our markets and to increase our market share, which may include strategic acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK

factors.summary: Construction activity is strong, driven by increased housing starts and repair and remodel demand.

evidence.text: U.S. total housing starts were 0.4 million for the first quarter of 2021, an increase of 10.2% compared to the first quarter of 2020. [...] Repair and remodeling activity generally continued to increase throughout our markets in the first quarter of 2021 despite the pandemic.
evidence.subsection: CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intense, with consolidation and significant competition from local and regional suppliers.

evidence.text: Our industry remains highly fragmented and competitive and we will continue to face significant competition from local and regional suppliers. [...] There has been a trend of consolidation within the building products supply industry.
evidence.subsection: COMPANY OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Low interest rates are acting as a tailwind, driving housing demand and benefiting the company's business.

evidence.text: Historically low interest rates, the aging of housing stock, and normal population growth [...] indicate U.S. housing demand will continue to grow. [...] The Company expects to see continued increases in housing demand over the next year.
evidence.subsection: COMPANY OVERVIEW

factors.summary: The company has successfully integrated the BMC Merger, which has contributed to revenue growth.

evidence.text: The BMC Merger significantly impacted net sales, contributing to a 79.5% increase.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: Cost actions are being taken, but the focus is on leveraging increased sales rather than cutting costs.

evidence.text: As a percentage of net sales, selling, general and administrative expenses decreased to 19.7% in 2021 from 22.6% in 2020, largely driven by cost leverage on increased net sales.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: Free cash flow is being negatively impacted by working capital increases.

evidence.text: Cash used in operating activities was $200.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $50.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020. The increase in cash used in operating activities was largely the result of a working capital increase...
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Cash Flows

factors.summary: Debt leverage is a significant factor, with interest expense decreasing due to lower debt in 2021.

evidence.text: Interest expense was $31.8 million in the first quarter of 2021, a decrease of $20.1 million from the first quarter of 2020. Interest expense decreased primarily due to a loss on extinguishment of $28.0 million recognized during the first quarter of 2020, partially offset by higher debt balances in the first quarter of 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: The company is repurchasing shares, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: The shares presented in the above table represent stock tendered in order to meet tax withholding requirements for restricted stock units vested.
evidence.subsection: Company Stock Repurchases

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by core sales growth in key markets. The increase is primarily due to core organic growth from sales volume increases in single-family and repair and remodel/other end markets.

evidence.text: Our net sales for the first quarter of 2021 increased 133.6% from the same period last year. [...] The remainder of the increase was a result of core organic sales growth in the single-family and repair and remodel/other end markets.
evidence.subsection: CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK

evidence.text: The remaining increase in sales is primarily attributable to core organic growth from sales volume increases in single-family and repair and remodel/other end markets, partially offset by one less selling day during the quarter.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, primarily driven by commodity price inflation, particularly affecting lumber prices. This increase is being partially offset through pricing adjustments.

evidence.text: Our gross profit percentage in the first quarter of 2021 decreased by 0.4% compared to the first quarter of 2020 primarily due to expense recognized during the three months ended March 31, 2021 in relation to the sell-through of inventory which was stepped up in value in connection with the BMC Merger. [...] Commodity price inflation accounted for another 31.3%.
evidence.subsection: CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK

evidence.text: The BMC Merger and commodity price inflation increased net sales by 79.5% and 31.3%, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: Overall demand is growing and accelerating, driven by strong housing starts, repair and remodel activity, and increased sales volumes across key end markets.

evidence.text: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. total housing starts were 0.4 million for the first quarter of 2021, an increase of 10.2% compared to the first quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK

evidence.text: The Home Improvement Research Institute is forecasting sales in the professional repair and remodel end market to increase approximately 15.5% in 2021 compared to 2020.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

evidence.text: We achieved increased sales in all of our product categories, primarily due to the BMC Merger, commodity inflation and higher sales volumes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed margin performance. While gross margins are increasing due to higher sales and a merger, the gross margin percentage is decreasing slightly due to inventory expenses. On the other hand, operating margins are improving because of cost leverage and decreased selling, general, and administrative expenses as a percentage of net sales.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $604.1 million to $1,069.6 million, driven primarily by the BMC Merger and sales growth. Our gross margin percentage decreased to 25.6% in the first quarter of 2021 from 26.0% in 2020, a 0.4% decrease.
evidence.subsection: CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK

evidence.text: Our gross profit percentage in the first quarter of 2021 decreased by 0.4% compared to the first quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses, as a percentage of net sales, were 19.7% in the first quarter of 2021, a 2.9% decrease from 22.6% in the first quarter of 2020, driven primarily by cost leverage on increased net sales.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, characterized by low interest rates and strong housing demand, which have contributed to increased sales across all product categories.

evidence.text: Historically low interest rates, the aging of housing stock, and normal population growth due to immigration and birthrate exceeding death rate indicate U.S. housing demand will continue to grow.
evidence.subsection: COMPANY OVERVIEW

evidence.text: The long-term outlook for the housing industry is positive due to growth in the underlying demographics compared to historical new construction levels.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020

evidence.text: We achieved increased sales in all of our product categories, primarily due to the BMC Merger, commodity inflation and higher sales volumes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2020
